title: Super Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back
content:
{{Top|rwm|reb|italics=1}} {{Redlink}} {{VideoGame |image=[[File:SuperEmpireStrikesBack.jpg]] |title=''Super Star Wars:
The Empire Strikes Back'' |developer=*[[LucasArts]] *[[Sculptured Software]] |publisher=*[[THQ]] *[[JVC|Victor]] (Japan) |release date=[[June 1]], [[1993]] {{C|[[Wikipedia:Super Nintendo Entertainment System|SNES]]}}''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]'' |closed= |upc= |engine= |genre=[[Wikipedia:Action game|Action]] |modes=[[Wikipedia:Single-player|Single player]] |ratings=[[Wikipedia:Entertainment Software Rating Board|ESRB]]: Everyone (E), [[Wikipedia:Entertainment and Leisure Software Publishers Association|ELSPA]]: 3+ |platforms=[[Wikipedia:Super Nintendo Entertainment System|Super Nintendo Entertainment System]], [[Wikipedia:Wii|Virtual Console]] |series= |basegame= |expansions= |director=[[Kalani Streicher]]{{WebCite|url=https://archive.org/details/super-star-wars-the-empire-strikes-back-usa-rev-1|text=''Super Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back'' Instruction Booklet|work=The Internet Archive|nobackup=1}} |producer= |designer=Kalani Streicher |programmer=[[Peter Ward]] and [[Ryan Ridges]] |artist=[[Harrison Fong]] (Art Supervisor) |writer= |composer=[[Paul Webb]] (Based on [[John Williams]]) |timeline= |prev=[[Super Star Wars|''Super Star Wars'']] |next=[[Super Star Wars: Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']] }} '''''Super Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back''''', sometimes called '''''Super The Empire Strikes Back''''' or simply '''''Super Empire Strikes Back''''' is a platform game for the [[Wikipedia:Super Nintendo Entertainment System|Super Nintendo]] (Super Famicom in Japan) which follows ''[[Super Star Wars]]'' and precedes ''[[Super Star Wars: Return of the Jedi]]''. It is largely based on the second film of the original ''[[Star Wars]]'' trilogy, [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']]. The game's manual added a number of names and details not spelled out in the game itself. The ''[[Super Empire Strikes Back Official Game Secrets]]'' guide released separately added even more details.''[[Super Empire Strikes Back Official Game Secrets]]'' ==Publisher's summary== '''''Will You Accept the Challenge of a Jedi Knight?''''' ''Pursued across the galaxy, you and your Rebel Forces now marshall new strength on the remote ice world of Hoth. Although tracked by probe droids, attacked by ice monsters, and confronted by an army of gigantic Imperial Walkers, you must not give in. You are the Alliance's only hope. Learn the ways of the Force, then test your skills in this fast-based, action-packed sequel to the hit game, Super Star Wars.'' ==Opening crawl== {{OpeningCrawl |name=Episode V
THE EMPIRE STRIKES BACK |text=It is a dark time for the Rebellion. Although the Death Star has been destroyed, Imperial troops have driven the Rebel forces from their hidden base and pursued them across the galaxy. Evading the dreaded Imperial Starfleet, a group of freedom fighters led by Luke Skywalker has established a new secret base on the remote ice world of Hoth. The evil lord Darth Vader, obsessed with finding young Skywalker, has dispatched thousands of remote probes into the far reaches of space....}} ==Gameplay== While the game roughly followed the plot of the film, several changes were made to accommodate gameplay. Characters fought their way from scene to scene and are confronted by multiple enemies. Other changes included [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] being carbon-frozen while searching for [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], rather than captured, and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] fighting [[Storm IV Twin-Pod cloud car/Legends|Cloud Cars]] in his [[T-65B X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing fighter]] while approaching [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]]. ''Super Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back'' followed closely the standard set by ''Super Star Wars'', with multiple playable characters and Mode 7 quasi-3D vehicle sequences. Controls were very similar to the first game, but with an enhanced double-jump. Unlike its predecessor, the player was able to use [[The Force/Legends|Force]] powers. Luke Skywalker, Han Solo, and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] were all playable characters. [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] and a [[Giant Wampa Creature|Giant Wampa]] appeared as bosses. The game included a password system so users could effectively save games without needing any onboard cartridge memory. ===Levels=== [[File:Empirestrikesbackgameplay.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] on [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]]] #[[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] 1 #Wampa Cave 1 - Boss: [[Giant Wampa Creature]] #Hoth 2 #Wampa Cave 2 #Hoth 3 - Boss: [[Viper probe droid/Legends|Imperial Probe Droid]] #[[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] 1 #[[Snowspeeder/Legends|Snowspeeder]] #Outside [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] #Inside AT-AT #Top of AT-AT - Boss: AT-AT Head #Echo Base 2 - Boss: C-A-T ([[Combat Attack Transport]]) #Echo Base 3 - Boss: [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]] #[[Hoth asteroid field/Legends|Asteroid Field]] #[[Dagobah/Legends|Dagobah]] 1 #Dagobah 2 #Dagobah 3 - Boss: [[Habogad|Swamp Creature Habogad]] #Cloud City 1 - Boss: [[Tibanna Hover Transport]] #Ugnaught Factory - Boss: [[Ugharro mining crusher]] #[[Carbon-freezing chamber/Legends|Carbon Freezing Chamber]] - Boss: Carbon Freezing Chamber Generator #Cloud City 2 - Boss: [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']] #X-Wing to Cloud City #Cloud City Reactor - Boss: [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] ===Weapons=== *[[Blaster/Legends|Laser blaster]] *[[Flame blaster]] *[[Proton Seeker]] *[[Rapid ion gun]] *[[Plasma Wave Blaster]] *[[Lightsaber/Legends|Lightsaber]] {{C|as Luke Skywalker only}} ===Enemies=== The game manual refers to some [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] by their singular names, but since multiple versions appear in one scene this list assumes they are more than one of the same species or droid model.
